article_title: UK and Norway Intercept Three Russian Submarines Surveying Undersea Cables in Month-Long North Atlantic Operation

content_preview.title: UK and Norway Intercept Three Russian Submarines Surveying Undersea Cables in Month-Long North Atlantic Operation
content_preview.summary: British and Norwegian forces tracked an Akula-class attack submarine and two GUGI deep-sea research vessels operating near critical undersea cable infrastructure north of the UK for over a month before the Russian fleet withdrew.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Britain's Defence Secretary John Healey revealed on April 9, 2026 that the Royal Navy and Norwegian armed forces conducted a joint operation lasting more than a month to track and deter three Russian submarines that were surveying undersea cables and pipelines in the North Atlantic, according to [Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/2026/04/09/uk-and-norway-foil-russian-submarine-plot-to-survey-undersea-cables-in-north-atlantic). The Russian flotilla, which included an Akula-class nuc...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong defense/security article on UK/Norway intercept of Russian submarines.
editor_notes.source_verification: Euronews VERIFIED via WebFetch: John Healey announcement, month-long operation, 1 attack sub + 2 spy vessels, undersea cables north of UK, Healey's quote, NATO context, Baltic November incidents, 13 warship UK-Norway partnership all confirmed.
